description: Deterministic dry-run migration output from Test Contract Registry v1 to v2. Generated by POST-H-003-B without replacing v1.

notes: The active v1 registry remains available for compatibility after POST-H-003-E; v2 is validated by quality-gate hardening as a local non-executing contract source.
All v2 classifications generated here are deterministic and marked as inferred or needs-review where appropriate.
POST-H-004-E adds explicit P0 contract for MiasiSemanticValidator while preserving v1 compatibility.

profiles.description: P0 contracts required for local hardening and release decisions.
Security-sensitive contracts, all local and explicit.
Contracts expected before local release packaging.
Contracts selected by changed watched paths.
Historical documentation regression contracts selected by documentation impact.

classification_notes: Migrated deterministically from v1 scope 'global-state' during POST-H-003-B.
Global-state contract is P0 because it owns centralized mutable metadata.

safety_exception.justification: Existing v1 global-state contract explicitly owns mutable project state metadata and is retained only as a local governance exception.
safety_exception.rollback_or_cleanup: Revert project-state metadata changes through Git and rerun project-state validate.

recommended_commands: python -m pytest tests/test_test_contract_registry.py tests/test_test_contract_registry_v2.py tests/test_test_contract_registry_migration.py tests/test_test_contract_registry_profiles_v2.py tests/test_test_impact.py tests/test_test_impact_v2.py tests/test_quality_gate.py tests/test_post_h_003_documentation.py -q
classification_notes: Migrated deterministically from v1 scope 'integration' during POST-H-003-B.
POST-H-003 closes Test Contract Registry 2.0 as a P0 local testing/governance contract with v1 compatibility.

recommended_commands: python -m pytest tests/test_miasi_semantic_validator.py tests/test_miasi_semantic_validator_fixtures.py tests/test_miasi_semantic_report_model.py tests/test_miasi_registry.py tests/test_schema_registry.py tests/test_quality_gate.py tests/test_post_h_004_documentation.py -q
classification_notes: POST-H-004-E closes the MIASI semantic validator hito as an implemented-initial local governance capability.
This contract validates report schema, unsafe fixtures, quality-gate integration and no-go semantics without executing agents, tools, evals or tests from JSON.

recommended_commands: python -m pytest tests/test_architecture_map_report.py tests/test_architecture_hotspots.py tests/test_architecture_dependencies.py tests/test_architecture_inventory.py tests/test_post_h_005_architecture_map.py tests/test_architecture_ownership_registry.py tests/test_schema_registry.py tests/test_quality_gate.py tests/test_project_global_state.py -q
python -m devpilot_core schema validate --schema-id ArchitectureMap --instance outputs/reports/architecture_map.json --json
classification_notes: POST-H-005-E closes the executable architecture map hito as an implemented-initial local architecture governance baseline.
The contract validates schema-backed reports, ownership gaps, hotspot evidence, quality-gate integration and no-go semantics without refactoring or executing external systems.

recommended_commands: python -m devpilot_core schema validate --schema-id CliCommandRegistry --instance outputs/reports/cli_command_registry.json --json
classification_notes: POST-H-006-A starts the CLI command registry hito as an implemented-initial read-only static inventory.
The contract validates schema-backed registry reports and safety invariants without migrating handlers or changing public command behavior.

recommended_commands: python -m devpilot_core schema validate --schema-id CliCommandRegistry --instance outputs/reports/cli_command_registry.json --json
classification_notes: POST-H-006-B adds an initial declarative overlay for governed CLI command groups without migrating handlers.
The contract validates descriptor completeness, coverage metrics, legacy-unregistered visibility and safety invariants without executing commands from the registry.

recommended_commands: python -m devpilot_core schema validate --schema-id CliCommandRegistry --instance outputs/reports/cli_command_registry.json --json
classification_notes: POST-H-006-C migrates selected CLI handlers into cli_commands modules while preserving public CLI wrappers.
Runtime registry routing and dynamic handler loading remain disabled; parity tests protect UX and CommandResult semantics.

recommended_commands: python -m devpilot_core schema validate --schema-id CliCommandRegistry --instance outputs/reports/cli_command_registry.json --json
classification_notes: POST-H-006-D produces a read-only CLI hotspot and ownership report derived from the CLI registry and Test Contract Registry.
The report is advisory and prepares POST-H-006-E no-growth enforcement and POST-H-007 ApplicationService boundary hardening.

recommended_commands: python -m devpilot_core schema validate --schema-id CliCommandRegistry --instance outputs/reports/cli_command_registry.json --json
classification_notes: POST-H-006-E blocks new legacy-unregistered CLI commands not present in the temporary allowlist.
The gate is local/read-only for sources and does not execute public commands or import handlers dynamically.

recommended_commands: python -m pytest tests/test_schema_registry.py tests/test_project_global_state.py tests/test_test_contract_registry.py tests/test_test_contract_registry_v2.py tests/test_test_contract_registry_profiles_v2.py -q
classification_notes: POST-H-007-A builds a read-only inventory of ApplicationService operations, API/UI mappings and CLI direct-core bypasses.
The sprint is advisory and does not change runtime behavior or enable remote/plugin/connector execution.
POST-H-007-B builds a schema-valid operation catalog from POST-H-007-A boundary inventory.
Catalog generation is declarative/read-only and does not change runtime routing or interface enforcement.
POST-H-007-C validates ApplicationRequest/ApplicationResponse runtime paths for 11 priority operations.
The sprint preserves CommandResult semantics and does not add HTTP routes, public CLI commands or interface enforcement.
POST-H-007-D applies initial interface-level guardrails inside ApplicationService.execute().
API/UI clients are strict; sensitive operations are policy-checked and dry-run guarded without adding public routes or commands.
POST-H-007-E relates CommandDescriptor metadata to ApplicationOperationCatalog operation_id values and adds a hardening quality-gate subgate.
Missing command-operation mappings are warnings in this initial version; API/UI operations without explicit contracts remain blocking.

recommended_commands: python -m pytest tests/test_runtime_state_policy_schema.py tests/test_schema_registry.py tests/test_project_global_state.py tests/test_test_contract_registry.py tests/test_test_contract_registry_v2.py tests/test_test_contract_registry_profiles_v2.py -q
classification_notes: POST-H-008-A defines runtime state taxonomy and policy schemas only; no cleanup execution is enabled.
The contract is P1/high because runtime artifacts can contain sensitive traces, DB state or agent sessions and clean ZIP hygiene is release-critical.
POST-H-008-B implements a read-only inventory and report generator; cleanup and export execution remain disabled.
The contract is P1/high because versioned runtime artifacts can leak sensitive traces, local DB state, agent sessions or generated outputs into clean release archives.
POST-H-008-C implements dry-run-first cleanup planning with guarded execution only for safe-cleanup artifacts.
The contract is P1/high because cleanup planning touches deletion semantics; tests enforce source-of-truth never-delete and confirmation-required execution.
POST-H-008-D implements local-only redacted runtime evidence export; dry-run remains default and execute writes only under outputs/runtime_exports/.
The contract is P1/high because runtime evidence may contain secrets, prompts, outputs or local operational state; tests enforce no raw prompt/output export and metadata-only DB handling.
POST-H-008-E adds a read-only runtime-state hygiene gate to hardening/industrial quality profiles and blocks dirty source/release archive candidates.
The contract is P1/high because versioned runtime artifacts, local DBs, agent sessions, caches or outputs in release ZIPs can leak sensitive state or corrupt source-of-truth packages.
POST-H-009-A creates a local canonical source registry for documentation governance.
The contract is P1/medium because documentation drift can mislead gates, roadmaps and release evidence, but this initial registry is read-only and has no runtime execution side effects.
POST-H-009-B introduces deterministic validation for frontmatter, status and ownership metadata declared in DocumentationSourceRegistry.
This contract is P1/medium because documentation governance failures can mislead release evidence, but the validator is local, read-only and does not run LLM judge or external services.
POST-H-009-C introduces deterministic Markdown/JSON synchronization checks for roadmap version, milestones, decisions, closure status and next hito.
This contract is P1/medium because documentation drift can mislead release evidence, but the validator is local, read-only and does not use LLM judge or external services.
POST-H-009-D extends documentation governance to executable backlogs derived from the post-H roadmap.
Missing future backlog files are planned informational findings, not blocking drift; existing backlog files must pass naming, frontmatter and milestone checks.
The validator is deterministic, read-only and local-first.
POST-H-009-E binds documentation governance to hardening/industrial quality gates as a deterministic read-only subgate.
The subgate blocks canonical-source, Markdown/JSON sync and backlog-governance drift without writing reports by default.
The integration uses no network, external APIs, LLM judge, source mutations or remote execution.

recommended_commands: python -m devpilot_core schema validate --schema-id PostHManifest --instance docs/post_h_010_a_manifest.json --json
classification_notes: POST-H-010-A defines observability retention policy defaults only; no inventory, cleanup, export or quality-gate integration is executed in this micro-sprint.
The policy is local-first, dry-run-first and blocks remote export/raw payload/secrets by contract.

recommended_commands: python -m devpilot_core schema validate --schema-id PostHManifest --instance docs/post_h_010_b_manifest.json --json
classification_notes: POST-H-010-B inventories observability runtime targets by metadata only; it does not cleanup, export, redact, call network or mutate sources.
Missing runtime targets in a clean source checkout are warnings and do not block local development gates.

recommended_commands: python -m pytest tests/test_observability_cleanup_plan.py tests/test_observability_inventory.py tests/test_post_h_010_observability_retention.py tests/test_schema_registry.py tests/test_project_global_state.py -q
python -m devpilot_core schema validate --schema-id PostHManifest --instance docs/post_h_010_c_manifest.json --json
classification_notes: POST-H-010-C generates a dry-run-only ObservabilityCleanupPlan with would_rotate/would_delete/would_archive/would_redact/would_export actions and no mutations.
Destructive actions embed PolicyEngine simulations and approval identifiers; actual cleanup/export execution remains disabled.

recommended_commands: python -m pytest tests/test_observability_export.py tests/test_observability_cleanup_plan.py tests/test_observability_inventory.py tests/test_post_h_010_observability_retention.py tests/test_schema_registry.py tests/test_project_global_state.py -q
python -m devpilot_core schema validate --schema-id PostHManifest --instance docs/post_h_010_d_manifest.json --json
classification_notes: POST-H-010-D exports local redacted observability evidence summaries and metadata under outputs/reports and outputs/audit_exports when --write-report is explicit.
Raw prompts, raw outputs, secrets, .env content, SQLite bytes, network, external APIs and remote export remain blocked.

notes: POST-H-010-E integrates observability-retention as a read-only quality-gate subgate without runtime-output dependency.
The gate validates policy, metadata inventory and clean ZIP hygiene without network, external APIs, raw payload reads or cleanup/export execution.
classification_notes: Local-first read-only observability retention hygiene quality-gate subgate; no network, no external APIs, no mutations and no raw payload reads.
POST-H-011-A is a local schema/fixture contract for RAG groundedness; it does not execute RAG, web search, external APIs or LLM judge.
Capability is high risk because false groundedness can mislead SDLC decisions, but this micro-sprint only defines testable fixtures.
POST-H-011-B validates citation/source coverage only; it does not execute LLM judge, web search, external APIs or remote connectors.
Capability is high risk because missing or stale evidence can mislead RAG-grounded SDLC decisions.
POST-H-011-C evaluates required and forbidden claims deterministically against local expected sources; it does not use LLM judge, web search, external APIs or remote connectors.
Capability is high risk because unsupported claims can mislead RAG-grounded SDLC decisions.
POST-H-011-D integrates local RAG query with deterministic source/claim groundedness without LLM judge, web search, external APIs or remote connectors.
Capability is high risk because RAG eval runner output can influence SDLC decisions; outputs/evals remain runtime artifacts only.
POST-H-011-E binds the local deterministic RAG groundedness runner to the hardening/industrial quality gate without external providers.
outputs/evals are runtime evidence and are not canonical versioned sources.
POST-H-012-A is declarative/validation-only; it blocks remote/plugin/connector execution and requires approval/RBAC metadata for critical actions.
POST-H-012-B is validation-only strong binding; it does not execute sensitive actions and does not enable remote/plugin/connector execution.
POST-H-012-C is a read-only exposure report; it does not grant access or execute sensitive actions.

recommended_commands: python -m pytest -p no:ddtrace tests/test_policy_engine_approval_rbac_enforcement.py tests/test_policy_engine.py tests/test_approval_binding.py tests/test_rbac_exposure.py -q
python -m devpilot_core schema validate --schema-id PostHManifest --instance docs/post_h_012_d_manifest.json --json
classification_notes: POST-H-012-D is PolicyEngine enforcement-only; it evaluates and blocks sensitive actions without enabling remote/plugin/connector execution.

notes: Validates approval-rbac-hardening subgate binding without executing sensitive actions.
Local-first, read-only and no external APIs.
classification_notes: POST-H-012-E is a read-only quality-gate subgate; it validates Approval/RBAC hardening without granting approvals or executing sensitive actions.
POST-H-013-A is schema/policy-only; it does not build, sign, encrypt or verify runtime audit pack ZIPs.
POST-H-013-B writes only generated pack artifacts under outputs/auditpacks when --execute is explicit; dry-run remains default and source mutations are forbidden.

POST-H-013-C verifies received local audit pack v2 ZIPs, writes only runtime integrity reports under outputs/auditpacks and blocks hash mismatch, missing file, extra member or invalid compliance claim.

recommended_commands: python -m devpilot_core audit-pack build-v2 --dry-run --sign optional --encrypt optional --json
classification_notes: POST-H-013-D adds optional local signing/encryption sidecars without KMS, network, external APIs or repo-stored keys.
Read-only local quality-gate contract for audit pack manifest policy, no-go gates, runbook and no-certification disclaimers.
Does not write packs, use network, external APIs, KMS, connector write, plugin execution or remote execution.
POST-H-014-A validates that every local FastAPI /api/v1 route is contract-registered with auth, policy, ApplicationService and no-go safety metadata.
The contract is read-only; it does not start the server, call network services, execute route handlers or mutate source files.

recommended_commands: python -m pytest -p no:ddtrace tests/test_post_h_014_response_mapping.py tests/test_api_local.py tests/test_api_security.py -q
classification_notes: POST-H-014-B validates explicit response mapping for ApplicationResponse HTTP payloads.
The tests are local-only and do not start a network server or call external APIs.

recommended_commands: python -m pytest -p no:ddtrace tests/test_post_h_014_ui_shell_contract.py tests/test_web_ui_mvp.py tests/test_web_ui_report_trace_viewer.py tests/test_web_ui_approval_center.py tests/test_web_ui_settings.py -q
classification_notes: POST-H-014-C validates critical Web UI page contracts, allowed API bindings, local/dry-run/no-remote badges and empty/error/loading state visibility.
